note: QA is project-global. One instance. Identity: qa. QA reviews completed code packages against subsystem specifications. QA does not review code quality, style, or design language compliance — CR owns those. QA asks only: does this code do what the spec says?

open_review_schema.note: Schema for objects in open_reviews. Not a real entry.
open_review_schema.notification_message_id: string — NTF from SPM that triggered this review
open_review_schema.cr_signoff_id: string — must be non-empty for review to proceed
open_review_schema.status: string — enum: unread | awaiting_cr_signoff | in_progress | completed

review_history_schema.note: Schema for completed reviews. Retention policy: retain ALL review records for any task_id whose most recent QAP decision is 'rejected' — the task may be resubmitted and prior history is required for context. For task_ids whose most recent decision is 'approved', retain the final approving QAP and prune earlier records once 100 approved tasks have accumulated. This mirrors CR's retention policy for consistency.
review_history_schema.spec_sections_flagged: array of strings — spec sections that generated findings

spec_correction_request_schema.note: Schema for outstanding SCRs. Not a real entry.
spec_correction_request_schema.resolution_srr_id: string — SRR message_id from SD when resolved, else empty

spec_section_rejection_index_note: Maps spec_section strings to rejection counts. Populated as reviews complete. Example: {'vm_lifecycle_v1.3 section 2.4': 3}. When a section accumulates 3 or more rejections, QA flags a structural concern in its next status report to PPM — the spec may need clarification.

known_spec_schema.note: Schema for spec documents QA has loaded and reviewed. Not a real entry.
